feature: Inventory and dependency graph: Clusters / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on Clusters cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
implementation_detail: Discover Clusters, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for Clusters.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for Clusters.
data_model: clusters_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Clusters cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Clusters; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Clusters.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for Clusters, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Clusters.
acceptance_criteria: Given Clusters data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Clusters connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Discover Clusters, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the scalability pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for Clusters.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for Clusters.
data_model: clusters_scalability_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Clusters scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Clusters; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Clusters.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Clusters recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Clusters.
acceptance_criteria: Given Clusters data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Clusters connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for Jobs.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for Jobs critical flows.
data_model: jobs_resilience_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Jobs resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Jobs; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Jobs.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Jobs recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Given Jobs data exists, when the resilience tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Jobs connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for CronJobs.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for CronJobs.
data_model: cronjobs_operational-excellence_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event

agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for CronJobs; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for CronJobs.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for CronJobs recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for CronJobs.
acceptance_criteria: Given CronJobs data exists, when the operational-excellence agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: CronJobs connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
